bullet_points: Load the dataset and filter for the 90 materials with elastic data.
Calibrate the decay constant $\alpha$ by scanning a range of values to maximize the separation between stable and metastable $V_{score}$ distributions.
Calculate $V_{score} = \text{percentile}(G_{vrh}) \times \exp(-\alpha \cdot \text{energy\_above\_hull})$ using the optimal $\alpha$.
Define the binary target `is_viable` as $V_{score} > \text{median}(V_{score})$ of the stable population.
Save the processed dataframe and print the chosen $\alpha$ with justification.
Generate and save a plot of the $V_{score}$ distribution split by `is_stable`.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data preparation and target definition for mechanical viability

bullet_points: Perform a Mann-Whitney U test comparing `energy_above_hull` and `volume_per_atom` for samples with and without `dos_at_fermi`.
Compare model performance (Random Forest, fixed hyperparameters) with and without `is_dos_missing` imputation (5th percentile) using ROC-AUC/balanced accuracy.
Calculate VIF for continuous features; flag and print features with VIF > 10.
Perform RFE (target 6–10 features) and save a plot of RFE cross-validation scores vs. number of features.
Save the selected feature list and the final training dataset.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Statistical analysis of missing data and feature selection

bullet_points: Train a Random Forest classifier using the selected features and `is_viable` target.
Implement LOGO cross-validation grouped by `M_group`; print sample counts per group.
Apply SMOTE to training folds; report ROC-AUC and balanced accuracy per fold and averaged.
Save the trained model and an aggregated confusion matrix plot.
Conduct SHAP dependence analysis for `d_band_filling` vs. `dos_at_fermi`.
Quantify the correlation between `M_soc_proxy` and `magnetic_ordering` using a Kruskal-Wallis test; print numerical results.
Generate and save SHAP summary and dependence plots.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Physical driver analysis and SHAP interpretation

bullet_points: Calculate Mahalanobis distance of metastable candidates to the centroid of the 90-sample elastic training population.
Generate a Pareto front plot (`energy_above_hull` vs. `predicted_viability_probability`) annotated by `phase` and color-coded by "chemically exotic" status (top 10th percentile distance).
Print the number of flagged candidates and save the plot.
sub_tasks.sub_task: OOD detection and Pareto front generation

bullet_points: Rank metastable candidates by `is_viable` probability.
Apply final filter: probability > 0.70 and `energy_above_hull` < 0.05 eV/atom.
Perform "leave-one-metal-out" sensitivity check; flag and exclude "sensitivity-dependent" candidates (probability drop < 0.70).
Print the final prioritized list (material_id, formula, phase, magnetic_ordering, energy_above_hull, predicted_viability_probability).
Interpret physical drivers of mechanical robustness (SHAP analysis) and the "synthesis window" (Pareto front).
Report all numerical cross-validation metrics (per-fold and averaged), feature importance, and OOD filtering thresholds.
Summarize sensitivity analysis outcomes and the final prioritized candidate list.
Write the full Results section, including a dedicated subsection on limitations (small elastic dataset, selection bias).
sub_tasks.sub_task: Synthesis of results and final report
